content: Just as human language learning relies on repeated explicit reflection (reflexion) on feedback to turn implicit errors into explicit mastery, large language models can draw on the graded, vocabulary-like progression of human reflexion practice to improve their own self-reflection mechanisms. Structured, spaced reflexion drills similar to those used in high school English vocabulary learning can help LLM self-reflection move from inconsistent, single-perspective debugging to consistent, multi-perspective error correction.
